<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cs:Fusako Takacukasa]][[en:Takatsukasa Fusako]][[fa:تاکاتسوکاسا فوساکو]][[fr:Fujiwara no Fusako]][[id:Fujiwara no Fusako]][[ja:鷹司房子]][[ko:다카쓰카사 후사코]][[sv:Fujiwara no Fusako]][[th:ทากัตสึกาซะ ฟูซาโกะ]][[vi:Takatsukasa Fusako]][[zh:鹰司房子]] {{Infobox person|birth_date={{birth date|1653|10|12}}|death_date={{death date|1712|5|19}}|gender={{ill|female|qid=Q6581072}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|qid=Q17}}; {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|qid=Q205662}}|title={{ill|Empress of Japan|qid=Q595038}}|spouse={{ill|Emperor Reigen|qid=Q313808}}|children={{ill|Masako-naishinnō|qid=Q7142051}}|father={{ill|Takatsukasa Norihira|qid=Q1742537}}|mother={{ill|Reizei Tamemitsus dotter|qid=Q109303450}}|relatives={{ill|Kujō Kaneharu|qid=Q3200258}}; {{ill|Takatsukasa Fusasuke|qid=Q924830}}; {{ill|Takatsukasa Nobuko|qid=Q7140666}}|given_name={{ill|Fusako|qid=Q5509874}}|resting_place={{ill|Tsuki no wa no misasagi|qid=Q3541649}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q4949213]] Wikidata name: Takatsukasa Fusako [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:45, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{family name hatnote|Takatsukasa|lang=Japanese}} {{Infobox royalty | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | succession = [[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress of Japan]] | reign = 1683–1687 | birth_name = | birth_date = {{birth date|1653|10|12|df=y}} | birth_place = | death_date = {{death date and age|1712|05|19|1653|10|12|df=y}} | death_place = | burial_date = | burial_place = Tsuki no wa no misasagi | spouse = {{marriage|[[:en:Emperor Reigen|Emperor Reigen]]|December 1, 1670}} | consort = yes | issue = | issue-link = | issue-pipe = | issue-type = | full name = | era name = | era dates = | regnal name = | posthumous name= | temple name = | royal house = [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] | father = Takatsukasa Norihira | mother = | religion = | occupation = | signature_type = | signature =
| module = }}
{{Nihongo|'''Takatsukasa Fusako'''|鷹司房子||October 12, 1653 – May 19, 1712}}, also known as {{Nihongo|'''Shinjōsaimon-in'''|新上西門院}}, was an empress consort of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. She was the consort of [[:en:Emperor Reigen|Emperor Reigen]].
==Life== Her father was [[:en:Takatsukasa Norihira|Takatsukasa Norihira]], who had the post of ''[[:en:Sadaijin|Sadaijin]]'' and the {{Nihongo|[[Ritsuryō#Establishment_of_court_Rank|junior first Rank]]|従一位|1=Junior First Rank}}. Her half-siblings by other mothers included the ''[[:en:Kampaku|Kampaku]]'' [[:en:Takatsukasa Fusasuke|Takatsukasa Fusasuke]], the ''[[:en:Sadaijin|Sadaijin]]'' [[:en:Kujō Kaneharu|Kujō Kaneharu]], and [[Takatsukasa Nobuko]], the wife of [[Tokugawa Tsunayoshi]]. Another theory holds that Fusako was actually Norihira's younger sister, whom he adopted as his daughter.
===Marriage=== On December 1, 1670,{{efn|The 21st day of the 11th month of [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 9.}} Fusako entered the court of Emperor Reigen, one year her junior, as a court lady. On June 23, 1673,{{efn|The 9th day of the 5th month of Kanbun 13.}} the palace went up in flames, and the estate of ''[[:en:Udaijin|Udaijin]]'' [[:en:Konoe Motohiro|Konoe Motohiro]] was used as a temporary palace. This was not a new occurrence: in 1661,{{efn|The 1st month of [[:en:Manji (era)|Manji]] 4.}} during the reign of the previous emperor [[:en:Emperor Go-Sai|Emperor Go-Sai]], another conflagration had led to the use of Motohiro's estate. On October 3, 1673,{{efn|The 23rd day of the 8th month of Kanbun 13.}} Fusako gave birth to her daughter {{Nihongo|Princess Masako|栄子内親王}}. In light of the great fire in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]] that had destroyed the palace, the [[:en:Japanese era name|era name]] was changed to [[:en:Enpō|Enpō]]. But on January 10, 1676,{{efn|The 25th day of the 11th month of Enpō 3.}} immediately after a new palace had been completed, the temporary one at Motohiro's estate caught flame in turn. Fusako and the emperor found refuge at the home of {{Nihongo|Yoshida Kaneyuki|吉田兼敬}} before entering the new palace two days later.
===Empress=== On January 1, 1683,{{efn|The 7th day of the 12th month of [[:en:Tenna|Tenna]] 2.}} Fusako was proclaimed {{Nihongo||准三后|jusangō}}, and on December 3{{efn|The 14th day of the 2nd month of Tenna 3.}} she was invested as ''[[:en:Chūgū|Chūgū]]''. She thereby became the emperor's legitimate wife—during the entire [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], this only happened four times. Reigen abdicated the throne on May 2, 1687,{{efn|The 21st day of the 3rd month of [[:en:Jōkyō|Jōkyō]] 4.}} in favor of Crown Prince Asahito, who then became [[:en:Emperor Higashiyama|Emperor Higashiyama]]. Accompanying this, Fusako was bestowed the honorary name Shinjōsaimon-in. In early 1695,{{efn|The 7th month of [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 8.}} the [[Tokugawa shogunate|shogunate]] presented her with 1,000 ''koku'' of land.
Takatsukasa Fusako died on May 19, 1712.{{efn|The 14th day of the 4th month of [[:en:Shōtoku (era)|Shōtoku]] 4.}} Her grave is located at [[:en:Tsuki no wa no misasagi|Tsuki no wa no misasagi]] in [[:en:Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|Higashiyama-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]].
==Notes== All Western dates calculated using Tsuchihashi's database via the [http://www.yukikurete.de/nengo_calc.htm Nengocalc] tool. {{notelist|30em}}
==References== {{reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[:en:Tokugawa Masako|Tokugawa Masako]] | title=[[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[:en:Princess Yukiko|Princess Yukiko]] | years=1683–1687}} {{s-end}}
{{Consorts of Japan|state=collapsed}}]]
{{-}} {{Japan-royal-stub}}

| module = }}
{{Nihongo|'''Takatsukasa Fusako'''|鷹司房子||October 12, 1653 – May 19, 1712}}, also known as {{Nihongo|'''Shinjōsaimon-in'''|新上西門院}}, was an empress consort of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. She was the consort of [[:en:Emperor Reigen|Emperor Reigen]].
==Life== Her father was [[:en:Takatsukasa Norihira|Takatsukasa Norihira]], who had the post of ''[[:en:Sadaijin|Sadaijin]]'' and the {{Nihongo|[[Ritsuryō#Establishment_of_court_Rank|junior first Rank]]|従一位|1=Junior First Rank}}. Her half-siblings by other mothers included the ''[[:en:Kampaku|Kampaku]]'' [[:en:Takatsukasa Fusasuke|Takatsukasa Fusasuke]], the ''[[:en:Sadaijin|Sadaijin]]'' [[:en:Kujō Kaneharu|Kujō Kaneharu]], and [[Takatsukasa Nobuko]], the wife of [[Tokugawa Tsunayoshi]]. Another theory holds that Fusako was actually Norihira's younger sister, whom he adopted as his daughter.
===Marriage=== On December 1, 1670,{{efn|The 21st day of the 11th month of [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 9.}} Fusako entered the court of Emperor Reigen, one year her junior, as a court lady. On June 23, 1673,{{efn|The 9th day of the 5th month of Kanbun 13.}} the palace went up in flames, and the estate of ''[[:en:Udaijin|Udaijin]]'' [[:en:Konoe Motohiro|Konoe Motohiro]] was used as a temporary palace. This was not a new occurrence: in 1661,{{efn|The 1st month of [[:en:Manji (era)|Manji]] 4.}} during the reign of the previous emperor [[:en:Emperor Go-Sai|Emperor Go-Sai]], another conflagration had led to the use of Motohiro's estate. On October 3, 1673,{{efn|The 23rd day of the 8th month of Kanbun 13.}} Fusako gave birth to her daughter {{Nihongo|Princess Masako|栄子内親王}}. In light of the great fire in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]] that had destroyed the palace, the [[:en:Japanese era name|era name]] was changed to [[:en:Enpō|Enpō]]. But on January 10, 1676,{{efn|The 25th day of the 11th month of Enpō 3.}} immediately after a new palace had been completed, the temporary one at Motohiro's estate caught flame in turn. Fusako and the emperor found refuge at the home of {{Nihongo|Yoshida Kaneyuki|吉田兼敬}} before entering the new palace two days later.
===Empress=== On January 1, 1683,{{efn|The 7th day of the 12th month of [[:en:Tenna|Tenna]] 2.}} Fusako was proclaimed {{Nihongo||准三后|jusangō}}, and on December 3{{efn|The 14th day of the 2nd month of Tenna 3.}} she was invested as ''[[:en:Chūgū|Chūgū]]''. She thereby became the emperor's legitimate wife—during the entire [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], this only happened four times. Reigen abdicated the throne on May 2, 1687,{{efn|The 21st day of the 3rd month of [[:en:Jōkyō|Jōkyō]] 4.}} in favor of Crown Prince Asahito, who then became [[:en:Emperor Higashiyama|Emperor Higashiyama]]. Accompanying this, Fusako was bestowed the honorary name Shinjōsaimon-in. In early 1695,{{efn|The 7th month of [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 8.}} the [[Tokugawa shogunate|shogunate]] presented her with 1,000 ''koku'' of land.
Takatsukasa Fusako died on May 19, 1712.{{efn|The 14th day of the 4th month of [[:en:Shōtoku (era)|Shōtoku]] 4.}} Her grave is located at [[:en:Tsuki no wa no misasagi|Tsuki no wa no misasagi]] in [[:en:Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|Higashiyama-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]].
==Notes== All Western dates calculated using Tsuchihashi's database via the [http://www.yukikurete.de/nengo_calc.htm Nengocalc] tool. {{notelist|30em}}
==References== {{reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[:en:Tokugawa Masako|Tokugawa Masako]] | title=[[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[:en:Princess Yukiko|Princess Yukiko]] | years=1683–1687}} {{s-end}}

{{Nihongo|'''Takatsukasa Fusako'''|鷹司房子||October 12, 1653 – May 19, 1712}}, also known as {{Nihongo|'''Shinjōsaimon-in'''|新上西門院}}, was an empress consort of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. She was the consort of [[:en:Emperor Reigen|Emperor Reigen]].
==Life== Her father was [[:en:Takatsukasa Norihira|Takatsukasa Norihira]], who had the post of ''[[:en:Sadaijin|Sadaijin]]'' and the {{Nihongo|[[Ritsuryō#Establishment_of_court_Rank|junior first Rank]]|従一位|1=Junior First Rank}}. Her half-siblings by other mothers included the ''[[:en:Kampaku|Kampaku]]'' [[:en:Takatsukasa Fusasuke|Takatsukasa Fusasuke]], the ''[[:en:Sadaijin|Sadaijin]]'' [[:en:Kujō Kaneharu|Kujō Kaneharu]], and [[Takatsukasa Nobuko]], the wife of [[Tokugawa Tsunayoshi]]. Another theory holds that Fusako was actually Norihira's younger sister, whom he adopted as his daughter.
===Marriage=== On December 1, 1670,{{efn|The 21st day of the 11th month of [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 9.}} Fusako entered the court of Emperor Reigen, one year her junior, as a court lady. On June 23, 1673,{{efn|The 9th day of the 5th month of Kanbun 13.}} the palace went up in flames, and the estate of ''[[:en:Udaijin|Udaijin]]'' [[:en:Konoe Motohiro|Konoe Motohiro]] was used as a temporary palace. This was not a new occurrence: in 1661,{{efn|The 1st month of [[:en:Manji (era)|Manji]] 4.}} during the reign of the previous emperor [[:en:Emperor Go-Sai|Emperor Go-Sai]], another conflagration had led to the use of Motohiro's estate. On October 3, 1673,{{efn|The 23rd day of the 8th month of Kanbun 13.}} Fusako gave birth to her daughter {{Nihongo|Princess Masako|栄子内親王}}. In light of the great fire in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]] that had destroyed the palace, the [[:en:Japanese era name|era name]] was changed to [[:en:Enpō|Enpō]]. But on January 10, 1676,{{efn|The 25th day of the 11th month of Enpō 3.}} immediately after a new palace had been completed, the temporary one at Motohiro's estate caught flame in turn. Fusako and the emperor found refuge at the home of {{Nihongo|Yoshida Kaneyuki|吉田兼敬}} before entering the new palace two days later.
===Empress=== On January 1, 1683,{{efn|The 7th day of the 12th month of [[:en:Tenna|Tenna]] 2.}} Fusako was proclaimed {{Nihongo||准三后|jusangō}}, and on December 3{{efn|The 14th day of the 2nd month of Tenna 3.}} she was invested as ''[[:en:Chūgū|Chūgū]]''. She thereby became the emperor's legitimate wife—during the entire [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], this only happened four times. Reigen abdicated the throne on May 2, 1687,{{efn|The 21st day of the 3rd month of [[:en:Jōkyō|Jōkyō]] 4.}} in favor of Crown Prince Asahito, who then became [[:en:Emperor Higashiyama|Emperor Higashiyama]]. Accompanying this, Fusako was bestowed the honorary name Shinjōsaimon-in. In early 1695,{{efn|The 7th month of [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 8.}} the [[Tokugawa shogunate|shogunate]] presented her with 1,000 ''koku'' of land.
Takatsukasa Fusako died on May 19, 1712.{{efn|The 14th day of the 4th month of [[:en:Shōtoku (era)|Shōtoku]] 4.}} Her grave is located at [[:en:Tsuki no wa no misasagi|Tsuki no wa no misasagi]] in [[:en:Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|Higashiyama-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]].
==Notes== All Western dates calculated using Tsuchihashi's database via the [http://www.yukikurete.de/nengo_calc.htm Nengocalc] tool. {{notelist|30em}}

| module = }}
{{Nihongo|'''Takatsukasa Fusako'''|鷹司房子||October 12, 1653 – May 19, 1712}}, also known as {{Nihongo|'''Shinjōsaimon-in'''|新上西門院}}, was an empress consort of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. She was the consort of [[:en:Emperor Reigen|Emperor Reigen]].
==Life== Her father was [[:en:Takatsukasa Norihira|Takatsukasa Norihira]], who had the post of ''[[:en:Sadaijin|Sadaijin]]'' and the {{Nihongo|[[Ritsuryō#Establishment_of_court_Rank|junior first Rank]]|従一位|1=Junior First Rank}}. Her half-siblings by other mothers included the ''[[:en:Kampaku|Kampaku]]'' [[:en:Takatsukasa Fusasuke|Takatsukasa Fusasuke]], the ''[[:en:Sadaijin|Sadaijin]]'' [[:en:Kujō Kaneharu|Kujō Kaneharu]], and [[Takatsukasa Nobuko]], the wife of [[Tokugawa Tsunayoshi]]. Another theory holds that Fusako was actually Norihira's younger sister, whom he adopted as his daughter.
===Marriage=== On December 1, 1670,{{efn|The 21st day of the 11th month of [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 9.}} Fusako entered the court of Emperor Reigen, one year her junior, as a court lady. On June 23, 1673,{{efn|The 9th day of the 5th month of Kanbun 13.}} the palace went up in flames, and the estate of ''[[:en:Udaijin|Udaijin]]'' [[:en:Konoe Motohiro|Konoe Motohiro]] was used as a temporary palace. This was not a new occurrence: in 1661,{{efn|The 1st month of [[:en:Manji (era)|Manji]] 4.}} during the reign of the previous emperor [[:en:Emperor Go-Sai|Emperor Go-Sai]], another conflagration had led to the use of Motohiro's estate. On October 3, 1673,{{efn|The 23rd day of the 8th month of Kanbun 13.}} Fusako gave birth to her daughter {{Nihongo|Princess Masako|栄子内親王}}. In light of the great fire in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]] that had destroyed the palace, the [[:en:Japanese era name|era name]] was changed to [[:en:Enpō|Enpō]]. But on January 10, 1676,{{efn|The 25th day of the 11th month of Enpō 3.}} immediately after a new palace had been completed, the temporary one at Motohiro's estate caught flame in turn. Fusako and the emperor found refuge at the home of {{Nihongo|Yoshida Kaneyuki|吉田兼敬}} before entering the new palace two days later.
===Empress=== On January 1, 1683,{{efn|The 7th day of the 12th month of [[:en:Tenna|Tenna]] 2.}} Fusako was proclaimed {{Nihongo||准三后|jusangō}}, and on December 3{{efn|The 14th day of the 2nd month of Tenna 3.}} she was invested as ''[[:en:Chūgū|Chūgū]]''. She thereby became the emperor's legitimate wife—during the entire [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], this only happened four times. Reigen abdicated the throne on May 2, 1687,{{efn|The 21st day of the 3rd month of [[:en:Jōkyō|Jōkyō]] 4.}} in favor of Crown Prince Asahito, who then became [[:en:Emperor Higashiyama|Emperor Higashiyama]]. Accompanying this, Fusako was bestowed the honorary name Shinjōsaimon-in. In early 1695,{{efn|The 7th month of [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 8.}} the [[Tokugawa shogunate|shogunate]] presented her with 1,000 ''koku'' of land.
Takatsukasa Fusako died on May 19, 1712.{{efn|The 14th day of the 4th month of [[:en:Shōtoku (era)|Shōtoku]] 4.}} Her grave is located at [[:en:Tsuki no wa no misasagi|Tsuki no wa no misasagi]] in [[:en:Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|Higashiyama-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]].
==Notes== All Western dates calculated using Tsuchihashi's database via the [http://www.yukikurete.de/nengo_calc.htm Nengocalc] tool. {{notelist|30em}}

{{Nihongo|'''Takatsukasa Fusako'''|鷹司房子||October 12, 1653 – May 19, 1712}}, also known as {{Nihongo|'''Shinjōsaimon-in'''|新上西門院}}, was an empress consort of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. She was the consort of [[:en:Emperor Reigen|Emperor Reigen]].
==Life== Her father was [[:en:Takatsukasa Norihira|Takatsukasa Norihira]], who had the post of ''[[:en:Sadaijin|Sadaijin]]'' and the {{Nihongo|[[Ritsuryō#Establishment_of_court_Rank|junior first Rank]]|従一位|1=Junior First Rank}}. Her half-siblings by other mothers included the ''[[:en:Kampaku|Kampaku]]'' [[:en:Takatsukasa Fusasuke|Takatsukasa Fusasuke]], the ''[[:en:Sadaijin|Sadaijin]]'' [[:en:Kujō Kaneharu|Kujō Kaneharu]], and [[Takatsukasa Nobuko]], the wife of [[Tokugawa Tsunayoshi]]. Another theory holds that Fusako was actually Norihira's younger sister, whom he adopted as his daughter.
===Marriage=== On December 1, 1670,{{efn|The 21st day of the 11th month of [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 9.}} Fusako entered the court of Emperor Reigen, one year her junior, as a court lady. On June 23, 1673,{{efn|The 9th day of the 5th month of Kanbun 13.}} the palace went up in flames, and the estate of ''[[:en:Udaijin|Udaijin]]'' [[:en:Konoe Motohiro|Konoe Motohiro]] was used as a temporary palace. This was not a new occurrence: in 1661,{{efn|The 1st month of [[:en:Manji (era)|Manji]] 4.}} during the reign of the previous emperor [[:en:Emperor Go-Sai|Emperor Go-Sai]], another conflagration had led to the use of Motohiro's estate. On October 3, 1673,{{efn|The 23rd day of the 8th month of Kanbun 13.}} Fusako gave birth to her daughter {{Nihongo|Princess Masako|栄子内親王}}. In light of the great fire in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]] that had destroyed the palace, the [[:en:Japanese era name|era name]] was changed to [[:en:Enpō|Enpō]]. But on January 10, 1676,{{efn|The 25th day of the 11th month of Enpō 3.}} immediately after a new palace had been completed, the temporary one at Motohiro's estate caught flame in turn. Fusako and the emperor found refuge at the home of {{Nihongo|Yoshida Kaneyuki|吉田兼敬}} before entering the new palace two days later.
===Empress=== On January 1, 1683,{{efn|The 7th day of the 12th month of [[:en:Tenna|Tenna]] 2.}} Fusako was proclaimed {{Nihongo||准三后|jusangō}}, and on December 3{{efn|The 14th day of the 2nd month of Tenna 3.}} she was invested as ''[[:en:Chūgū|Chūgū]]''. She thereby became the emperor's legitimate wife—during the entire [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], this only happened four times. Reigen abdicated the throne on May 2, 1687,{{efn|The 21st day of the 3rd month of [[:en:Jōkyō|Jōkyō]] 4.}} in favor of Crown Prince Asahito, who then became [[:en:Emperor Higashiyama|Emperor Higashiyama]]. Accompanying this, Fusako was bestowed the honorary name Shinjōsaimon-in. In early 1695,{{efn|The 7th month of [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 8.}} the [[Tokugawa shogunate|shogunate]] presented her with 1,000 ''koku'' of land.
Takatsukasa Fusako died on May 19, 1712.{{efn|The 14th day of the 4th month of [[:en:Shōtoku (era)|Shōtoku]] 4.}} Her grave is located at [[:en:Tsuki no wa no misasagi|Tsuki no wa no misasagi]] in [[:en:Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|Higashiyama-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]].
==Notes== All Western dates calculated using Tsuchihashi's database via the [http://www.yukikurete.de/nengo_calc.htm Nengocalc] tool. {{notelist|30em}}
==References== {{reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[:en:Tokugawa Masako|Tokugawa Masako]] | title=[[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[:en:Princess Yukiko|Princess Yukiko]] | years=1683–1687}} {{s-end}}
{{Consorts of Japan|state=collapsed}}]]

{{Nihongo|'''Takatsukasa Fusako'''|鷹司房子||October 12, 1653 – May 19, 1712}}, also known as {{Nihongo|'''Shinjōsaimon-in'''|新上西門院}}, was an empress consort of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. She was the consort of [[:en:Emperor Reigen|Emperor Reigen]].
==Life== Her father was [[:en:Takatsukasa Norihira|Takatsukasa Norihira]], who had the post of ''[[:en:Sadaijin|Sadaijin]]'' and the {{Nihongo|[[Ritsuryō#Establishment_of_court_Rank|junior first Rank]]|従一位|1=Junior First Rank}}. Her half-siblings by other mothers included the ''[[:en:Kampaku|Kampaku]]'' [[:en:Takatsukasa Fusasuke|Takatsukasa Fusasuke]], the ''[[:en:Sadaijin|Sadaijin]]'' [[:en:Kujō Kaneharu|Kujō Kaneharu]], and [[Takatsukasa Nobuko]], the wife of [[Tokugawa Tsunayoshi]]. Another theory holds that Fusako was actually Norihira's younger sister, whom he adopted as his daughter.
===Marriage=== On December 1, 1670,{{efn|The 21st day of the 11th month of [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 9.}} Fusako entered the court of Emperor Reigen, one year her junior, as a court lady. On June 23, 1673,{{efn|The 9th day of the 5th month of Kanbun 13.}} the palace went up in flames, and the estate of ''[[:en:Udaijin|Udaijin]]'' [[:en:Konoe Motohiro|Konoe Motohiro]] was used as a temporary palace. This was not a new occurrence: in 1661,{{efn|The 1st month of [[:en:Manji (era)|Manji]] 4.}} during the reign of the previous emperor [[:en:Emperor Go-Sai|Emperor Go-Sai]], another conflagration had led to the use of Motohiro's estate. On October 3, 1673,{{efn|The 23rd day of the 8th month of Kanbun 13.}} Fusako gave birth to her daughter {{Nihongo|Princess Masako|栄子内親王}}. In light of the great fire in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]] that had destroyed the palace, the [[:en:Japanese era name|era name]] was changed to [[:en:Enpō|Enpō]]. But on January 10, 1676,{{efn|The 25th day of the 11th month of Enpō 3.}} immediately after a new palace had been completed, the temporary one at Motohiro's estate caught flame in turn. Fusako and the emperor found refuge at the home of {{Nihongo|Yoshida Kaneyuki|吉田兼敬}} before entering the new palace two days later.
===Empress=== On January 1, 1683,{{efn|The 7th day of the 12th month of [[:en:Tenna|Tenna]] 2.}} Fusako was proclaimed {{Nihongo||准三后|jusangō}}, and on December 3{{efn|The 14th day of the 2nd month of Tenna 3.}} she was invested as ''[[:en:Chūgū|Chūgū]]''. She thereby became the emperor's legitimate wife—during the entire [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], this only happened four times. Reigen abdicated the throne on May 2, 1687,{{efn|The 21st day of the 3rd month of [[:en:Jōkyō|Jōkyō]] 4.}} in favor of Crown Prince Asahito, who then became [[:en:Emperor Higashiyama|Emperor Higashiyama]]. Accompanying this, Fusako was bestowed the honorary name Shinjōsaimon-in. In early 1695,{{efn|The 7th month of [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 8.}} the [[Tokugawa shogunate|shogunate]] presented her with 1,000 ''koku'' of land.
Takatsukasa Fusako died on May 19, 1712.{{efn|The 14th day of the 4th month of [[:en:Shōtoku (era)|Shōtoku]] 4.}} Her grave is located at [[:en:Tsuki no wa no misasagi|Tsuki no wa no misasagi]] in [[:en:Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|Higashiyama-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]].
==Notes== All Western dates calculated using Tsuchihashi's database via the [http://www.yukikurete.de/nengo_calc.htm Nengocalc] tool. {{notelist|30em}}

On October 3, 1673,{{efn|The 23rd day of the 8th month of Kanbun 13.}} Fusako gave birth to her daughter {{Nihongo|Princess Masako|栄子内親王}}. In light of the great fire in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]] that had destroyed the palace, the [[:en:Japanese era name|era name]] was changed to [[:en:Enpō|Enpō]]. But on January 10, 1676,{{efn|The 25th day of the 11th month of Enpō 3.}} immediately after a new palace had been completed, the temporary one at Motohiro's estate caught flame in turn. Fusako and the emperor found refuge at the home of {{Nihongo|Yoshida Kaneyuki|吉田兼敬}} before entering the new palace two days later.
===Empress=== On January 1, 1683,{{efn|The 7th day of the 12th month of [[:en:Tenna|Tenna]] 2.}} Fusako was proclaimed {{Nihongo||准三后|jusangō}}, and on December 3{{efn|The 14th day of the 2nd month of Tenna 3.}} she was invested as ''[[:en:Chūgū|Chūgū]]''. She thereby became the emperor's legitimate wife—during the entire [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], this only happened four times. Reigen abdicated the throne on May 2, 1687,{{efn|The 21st day of the 3rd month of [[:en:Jōkyō|Jōkyō]] 4.}} in favor of Crown Prince Asahito, who then became [[:en:Emperor Higashiyama|Emperor Higashiyama]]. Accompanying this, Fusako was bestowed the honorary name Shinjōsaimon-in. In early 1695,{{efn|The 7th month of [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 8.}} the [[Tokugawa shogunate|shogunate]] presented her with 1,000 ''koku'' of land.
Takatsukasa Fusako died on May 19, 1712.{{efn|The 14th day of the 4th month of [[:en:Shōtoku (era)|Shōtoku]] 4.}} Her grave is located at [[:en:Tsuki no wa no misasagi|Tsuki no wa no misasagi]] in [[:en:Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|Higashiyama-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]].
==Notes== All Western dates calculated using Tsuchihashi's database via the [http://www.yukikurete.de/nengo_calc.htm Nengocalc] tool. {{notelist|30em}}

{{Nihongo|'''Takatsukasa Fusako'''|鷹司房子||October 12, 1653 – May 19, 1712}}, also known as {{Nihongo|'''Shinjōsaimon-in'''|新上西門院}}, was an empress consort of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. She was the consort of {{ill|Emperor Reigen|en|Emperor Reigen|WD=Q313808|lt=Emperor Reigen}}.
==Life== Her father was {{ill|Takatsukasa Norihira|en|Takatsukasa Norihira|WD=Q1742537|lt=Takatsukasa Norihira}}, who had the post of ''[[:en:Sadaijin|Sadaijin]]'' and the {{Nihongo|[[Ritsuryō#Establishment_of_court_Rank|junior first Rank]]|従一位|1=Junior First Rank}}. Her half-siblings by other mothers included the ''[[:en:Kampaku|Kampaku]]'' {{ill|Takatsukasa Fusasuke|en|Takatsukasa Fusasuke|WD=Q924830|lt=Takatsukasa Fusasuke}}, the ''[[:en:Sadaijin|Sadaijin]]'' {{ill|Kujō Kaneharu|en|Kujō Kaneharu|WD=Q3200258|lt=Kujō Kaneharu}}, and [[Takatsukasa Nobuko]], the wife of [[Tokugawa Tsunayoshi]]. Another theory holds that Fusako was actually Norihira's younger sister, whom he adopted as his daughter.
===Marriage=== On December 1, 1670,{{efn|The 21st day of the 11th month of {{ill|Kanbun (era)|en|Kanbun (era)|WD=Q1146228|lt=Kanbun}} 9.}} Fusako entered the court of Emperor Reigen, one year her junior, as a court lady. On June 23, 1673,{{efn|The 9th day of the 5th month of Kanbun 13.}} the palace went up in flames, and the estate of ''[[:en:Udaijin|Udaijin]]'' {{ill|Konoe Motohiro|en|Konoe Motohiro|WD=Q922862|lt=Konoe Motohiro}} was used as a temporary palace. This was not a new occurrence: in 1661,{{efn|The 1st month of {{ill|Manji (era)|en|Manji (era)|WD=Q1145295|lt=Manji}} 4.}} during the reign of the previous emperor {{ill|Emperor Go-Sai|en|Emperor Go-Sai|WD=Q349414|lt=Emperor Go-Sai}}, another conflagration had led to the use of Motohiro's estate. On October 3, 1673,{{efn|The 23rd day of the 8th month of Kanbun 13.}} Fusako gave birth to her daughter {{Nihongo|Princess Masako|栄子内親王}}. In light of the great fire in {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto}} that had destroyed the palace, the {{ill|Japanese era name|en|Japanese era name|WD=Q24706|lt=era name}} was changed to {{ill|Enpō|en|Enpō|WD=Q1145735|lt=Enpō}}. But on January 10, 1676,{{efn|The 25th day of the 11th month of Enpō 3.}} immediately after a new palace had been completed, the temporary one at Motohiro's estate caught flame in turn. Fusako and the emperor found refuge at the home of {{Nihongo|Yoshida Kaneyuki|吉田兼敬}} before entering the new palace two days later.
===Empress=== On January 1, 1683,{{efn|The 7th day of the 12th month of {{ill|Tenna|en|Tenna|WD=Q1145289|lt=Tenna}} 2.}} Fusako was proclaimed {{Nihongo||准三后|jusangō}}, and on December 3{{efn|The 14th day of the 2nd month of Tenna 3.}} she was invested as ''[[:en:Chūgū|Chūgū]]''. She thereby became the emperor's legitimate wife—during the entire {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, this only happened four times. Reigen abdicated the throne on May 2, 1687,{{efn|The 21st day of the 3rd month of {{ill|Jōkyō|en|Jōkyō|WD=Q1146221|lt=Jōkyō}} 4.}} in favor of Crown Prince Asahito, who then became {{ill|Emperor Higashiyama|en|Emperor Higashiyama|WD=Q313801|lt=Emperor Higashiyama}}. Accompanying this, Fusako was bestowed the honorary name Shinjōsaimon-in. In early 1695,{{efn|The 7th month of {{ill|Genroku|en|Genroku|WD=Q1325001|lt=Genroku}} 8.}} the [[Tokugawa shogunate|shogunate]] presented her with 1,000 ''koku'' of land.
Takatsukasa Fusako died on May 19, 1712.{{efn|The 14th day of the 4th month of {{ill|Shōtoku (era)|en|Shōtoku (era)|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shōtoku}} 4.}} Her grave is located at {{ill|Tsuki no wa no misasagi|en|Tsuki no wa no misasagi|WD=Q3541649|lt=Tsuki no wa no misasagi}} in {{ill|Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|en|Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1203862|lt=Higashiyama-ku}}, {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto}}.

{{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|WD=Q205662}}|title={{ill|Empress of Japan|WD=Q595038}}|spouse={{ill|Emperor Reigen|WD=Q313808}}|children={{ill|Masako-naishinnō|WD=Q7142051}}|father={{ill|Takatsukasa Norihira|WD=Q1742537}}|mother={{ill|Reizei Tamemitsus dotter|WD=Q109303450}}|relatives={{ill|Kujō Kaneharu|WD=Q3200258}}; {{ill|Takatsukasa Fusasuke|WD=Q924830}}; {{ill|Takatsukasa Nobuko|WD=Q7140666}}|given_name={{ill|Fusako|WD=Q5509874}}|resting_place={{ill|Tsuki no wa no misasagi|WD=Q3541649}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q4949213]] Wikidata name: Takatsukasa Fusako [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:45, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{family name hatnote|Takatsukasa|lang=Japanese}} {{Infobox royalty | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | succession = {{ill|Empress of Japan|en|Empress of Japan|WD=Q595038|lt=Empress of Japan}} | reign = 1683–1687 | birth_name = | birth_date = {{birth date|1653|10|12|df=y}} | birth_place = | death_date = {{death date and age|1712|05|19|1653|10|12|df=y}} | death_place = | burial_date = | burial_place = Tsuki no wa no misasagi | spouse = {{marriage|{{ill|Emperor Reigen|en|Emperor Reigen|WD=Q313808|lt=Emperor Reigen}}|December 1, 1670}} | consort = yes | issue = | issue-link = | issue-pipe = | issue-type = | full name = | era name = | era dates = | regnal name = | posthumous name= | temple name = | royal house = {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=Imperial House of Japan}} | father = Takatsukasa Norihira | mother = | religion = | occupation = | signature_type = | signature =
| module = }}
{{Nihongo|'''Takatsukasa Fusako'''|鷹司房子||October 12, 1653 – May 19, 1712}}, also known as {{Nihongo|'''Shinjōsaimon-in'''|新上西門院}}, was an empress consort of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. She was the consort of {{ill|Emperor Reigen|en|Emperor Reigen|WD=Q313808|lt=Emperor Reigen}}.
==Life== Her father was {{ill|Takatsukasa Norihira|en|Takatsukasa Norihira|WD=Q1742537|lt=Takatsukasa Norihira}}, who had the post of ''[[:en:Sadaijin|Sadaijin]]'' and the {{Nihongo|[[Ritsuryō#Establishment_of_court_Rank|junior first Rank]]|従一位|1=Junior First Rank}}. Her half-siblings by other mothers included the ''[[:en:Kampaku|Kampaku]]'' {{ill|Takatsukasa Fusasuke|en|Takatsukasa Fusasuke|WD=Q924830|lt=Takatsukasa Fusasuke}}, the ''[[:en:Sadaijin|Sadaijin]]'' {{ill|Kujō Kaneharu|en|Kujō Kaneharu|WD=Q3200258|lt=Kujō Kaneharu}}, and [[Takatsukasa Nobuko]], the wife of [[Tokugawa Tsunayoshi]]. Another theory holds that Fusako was actually Norihira's younger sister, whom he adopted as his daughter.
===Marriage=== On December 1, 1670,{{efn|The 21st day of the 11th month of {{ill|Kanbun (era)|en|Kanbun (era)|WD=Q1146228|lt=Kanbun}} 9.}} Fusako entered the court of Emperor Reigen, one year her junior, as a court lady. On June 23, 1673,{{efn|The 9th day of the 5th month of Kanbun 13.}} the palace went up in flames, and the estate of ''[[:en:Udaijin|Udaijin]]'' {{ill|Konoe Motohiro|en|Konoe Motohiro|WD=Q922862|lt=Konoe Motohiro}} was used as a temporary palace. This was not a new occurrence: in 1661,{{efn|The 1st month of {{ill|Manji (era)|en|Manji (era)|WD=Q1145295|lt=Manji}} 4.}} during the reign of the previous emperor {{ill|Emperor Go-Sai|en|Emperor Go-Sai|WD=Q349414|lt=Emperor Go-Sai}}, another conflagration had led to the use of Motohiro's estate. On October 3, 1673,{{efn|The 23rd day of the 8th month of Kanbun 13.}} Fusako gave birth to her daughter {{Nihongo|Princess Masako|栄子内親王}}. In light of the great fire in {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto}} that had destroyed the palace, the {{ill|Japanese era name|en|Japanese era name|WD=Q24706|lt=era name}} was changed to {{ill|Enpō|en|Enpō|WD=Q1145735|lt=Enpō}}. But on January 10, 1676,{{efn|The 25th day of the 11th month of Enpō 3.}} immediately after a new palace had been completed, the temporary one at Motohiro's estate caught flame in turn. Fusako and the emperor found refuge at the home of {{Nihongo|Yoshida Kaneyuki|吉田兼敬}} before entering the new palace two days later.
===Empress=== On January 1, 1683,{{efn|The 7th day of the 12th month of {{ill|Tenna|en|Tenna|WD=Q1145289|lt=Tenna}} 2.}} Fusako was proclaimed {{Nihongo||准三后|jusangō}}, and on December 3{{efn|The 14th day of the 2nd month of Tenna 3.}} she was invested as ''[[:en:Chūgū|Chūgū]]''. She thereby became the emperor's legitimate wife—during the entire {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, this only happened four times. Reigen abdicated the throne on May 2, 1687,{{efn|The 21st day of the 3rd month of {{ill|Jōkyō|en|Jōkyō|WD=Q1146221|lt=Jōkyō}} 4.}} in favor of Crown Prince Asahito, who then became {{ill|Emperor Higashiyama|en|Emperor Higashiyama|WD=Q313801|lt=Emperor Higashiyama}}. Accompanying this, Fusako was bestowed the honorary name Shinjōsaimon-in. In early 1695,{{efn|The 7th month of {{ill|Genroku|en|Genroku|WD=Q1325001|lt=Genroku}} 8.}} the [[Tokugawa shogunate|shogunate]] presented her with 1,000 ''koku'' of land.
Takatsukasa Fusako died on May 19, 1712.{{efn|The 14th day of the 4th month of {{ill|Shōtoku (era)|en|Shōtoku (era)|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shōtoku}} 4.}} Her grave is located at {{ill|Tsuki no wa no misasagi|en|Tsuki no wa no misasagi|WD=Q3541649|lt=Tsuki no wa no misasagi}} in {{ill|Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|en|Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1203862|lt=Higashiyama-ku}}, {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto}}.
==Notes== All Western dates calculated using Tsuchihashi's database via the [http://www.yukikurete.de/nengo_calc.htm Nengocalc] tool. {{notelist|30em}}

{{Nihongo|'''Takatsukasa Fusako'''|鷹司房子||October 12, 1653 – May 19, 1712}}, also known as {{Nihongo|'''Shinjōsaimon-in'''|新上西門院}}, was an empress consort of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. She was the consort of {{ill|Emperor Reigen|en|Emperor Reigen|WD=Q313808|lt=Emperor Reigen}}.
==Life== Her father was {{ill|Takatsukasa Norihira|en|Takatsukasa Norihira|WD=Q1742537|lt=Takatsukasa Norihira}}, who had the post of ''[[:en:Sadaijin|Sadaijin]]'' and the {{Nihongo|[[Ritsuryō#Establishment_of_court_Rank|junior first Rank]]|従一位|1=Junior First Rank}}. Her half-siblings by other mothers included the ''[[:en:Kampaku|Kampaku]]'' {{ill|Takatsukasa Fusasuke|en|Takatsukasa Fusasuke|WD=Q924830|lt=Takatsukasa Fusasuke}}, the ''[[:en:Sadaijin|Sadaijin]]'' {{ill|Kujō Kaneharu|en|Kujō Kaneharu|WD=Q3200258|lt=Kujō Kaneharu}}, and [[Takatsukasa Nobuko]], the wife of [[Tokugawa Tsunayoshi]]. Another theory holds that Fusako was actually Norihira's younger sister, whom he adopted as his daughter.
===Marriage=== On December 1, 1670,{{efn|The 21st day of the 11th month of {{ill|Kanbun (era)|en|Kanbun (era)|WD=Q1146228|lt=Kanbun}} 9.}} Fusako entered the court of Emperor Reigen, one year her junior, as a court lady. On June 23, 1673,{{efn|The 9th day of the 5th month of Kanbun 13.}} the palace went up in flames, and the estate of ''[[:en:Udaijin|Udaijin]]'' {{ill|Konoe Motohiro|en|Konoe Motohiro|WD=Q922862|lt=Konoe Motohiro}} was used as a temporary palace. This was not a new occurrence: in 1661,{{efn|The 1st month of {{ill|Manji (era)|en|Manji (era)|WD=Q1145295|lt=Manji}} 4.}} during the reign of the previous emperor {{ill|Emperor Go-Sai|en|Emperor Go-Sai|WD=Q349414|lt=Emperor Go-Sai}}, another conflagration had led to the use of Motohiro's estate. On October 3, 1673,{{efn|The 23rd day of the 8th month of Kanbun 13.}} Fusako gave birth to her daughter {{Nihongo|Princess Masako|栄子内親王}}. In light of the great fire in {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto}} that had destroyed the palace, the {{ill|Japanese era name|en|Japanese era name|WD=Q24706|lt=era name}} was changed to {{ill|Enpō|en|Enpō|WD=Q1145735|lt=Enpō}}. But on January 10, 1676,{{efn|The 25th day of the 11th month of Enpō 3.}} immediately after a new palace had been completed, the temporary one at Motohiro's estate caught flame in turn. Fusako and the emperor found refuge at the home of {{Nihongo|Yoshida Kaneyuki|吉田兼敬}} before entering the new palace two days later.

She thereby became the emperor's legitimate wife—during the entire {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, this only happened four times. Reigen abdicated the throne on May 2, 1687,{{efn|The 21st day of the 3rd month of {{ill|Jōkyō|en|Jōkyō|WD=Q1146221|lt=Jōkyō}} 4.}} in favor of Crown Prince Asahito, who then became {{ill|Emperor Higashiyama|en|Emperor Higashiyama|WD=Q313801|lt=Emperor Higashiyama}}. Accompanying this, Fusako was bestowed the honorary name Shinjōsaimon-in. In early 1695,{{efn|The 7th month of {{ill|Genroku|en|Genroku|WD=Q1325001|lt=Genroku}} 8.}} the [[Tokugawa shogunate|shogunate]] presented her with 1,000 ''koku'' of land.
Takatsukasa Fusako died on May 19, 1712.{{efn|The 14th day of the 4th month of {{ill|Shōtoku (era)|en|Shōtoku (era)|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shōtoku}} 4.}} Her grave is located at {{ill|Tsuki no wa no misasagi|en|Tsuki no wa no misasagi|WD=Q3541649|lt=Tsuki no wa no misasagi}} in {{ill|Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|en|Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1203862|lt=Higashiyama-ku}}, {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto}}.

{{Nihongo|'''Takatsukasa Fusako'''|鷹司房子||October 12, 1653 – May 19, 1712}}, also known as {{Nihongo|'''Shinjōsaimon-in'''|新上西門院}}, was an empress consort of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. She was the consort of {{ill|Emperor Reigen|en|Emperor Reigen|WD=Q313808|lt=Emperor Reigen}}.
==Life== Her father was {{ill|Takatsukasa Norihira|en|Takatsukasa Norihira|WD=Q1742537|lt=Takatsukasa Norihira}}, who had the post of ''[[:en:Sadaijin|Sadaijin]]'' and the {{Nihongo|[[Ritsuryō#Establishment_of_court_Rank|junior first Rank]]|従一位|1=Junior First Rank}}. Her half-siblings by other mothers included the ''[[:en:Kampaku|Kampaku]]'' {{ill|Takatsukasa Fusasuke|en|Takatsukasa Fusasuke|WD=Q924830|lt=Takatsukasa Fusasuke}}, the ''[[:en:Sadaijin|Sadaijin]]'' {{ill|Kujō Kaneharu|en|Kujō Kaneharu|WD=Q3200258|lt=Kujō Kaneharu}}, and [[Takatsukasa Nobuko]], the wife of [[Tokugawa Tsunayoshi]]. Another theory holds that Fusako was actually Norihira's younger sister, whom he adopted as his daughter.
===Marriage=== On December 1, 1670,{{efn|The 21st day of the 11th month of {{ill|Kanbun (era)|en|Kanbun (era)|WD=Q1146228|lt=Kanbun}} 9.}} Fusako entered the court of Emperor Reigen, one year her junior, as a court lady. On June 23, 1673,{{efn|The 9th day of the 5th month of Kanbun 13.}} the palace went up in flames, and the estate of ''[[:en:Udaijin|Udaijin]]'' {{ill|Konoe Motohiro|en|Konoe Motohiro|WD=Q922862|lt=Konoe Motohiro}} was used as a temporary palace. This was not a new occurrence: in 1661,{{efn|The 1st month of {{ill|Manji (era)|en|Manji (era)|WD=Q1145295|lt=Manji}} 4.}} during the reign of the previous emperor {{ill|Emperor Go-Sai|en|Emperor Go-Sai|WD=Q349414|lt=Emperor Go-Sai}}, another conflagration had led to the use of Motohiro's estate. On October 3, 1673,{{efn|The 23rd day of the 8th month of Kanbun 13.}} Fusako gave birth to her daughter {{Nihongo|Princess Masako|栄子内親王}}. In light of the great fire in {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto}} that had destroyed the palace, the {{ill|Japanese era name|en|Japanese era name|WD=Q24706|lt=era name}} was changed to {{ill|Enpō|en|Enpō|WD=Q1145735|lt=Enpō}}. But on January 10, 1676,{{efn|The 25th day of the 11th month of Enpō 3.}} immediately after a new palace had been completed, the temporary one at Motohiro's estate caught flame in turn. Fusako and the emperor found refuge at the home of {{Nihongo|Yoshida Kaneyuki|吉田兼敬}} before entering the new palace two days later.
===Empress=== On January 1, 1683,{{efn|The 7th day of the 12th month of {{ill|Tenna|en|Tenna|WD=Q1145289|lt=Tenna}} 2.}} Fusako was proclaimed {{Nihongo||准三后|jusangō}}, and on December 3{{efn|The 14th day of the 2nd month of Tenna 3.}} she was invested as ''[[:en:Chūgū|Chūgū]]''. She thereby became the emperor's legitimate wife—during the entire {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, this only happened four times. Reigen abdicated the throne on May 2, 1687,{{efn|The 21st day of the 3rd month of {{ill|Jōkyō|en|Jōkyō|WD=Q1146221|lt=Jōkyō}} 4.}} in favor of Crown Prince Asahito, who then became {{ill|Emperor Higashiyama|en|Emperor Higashiyama|WD=Q313801|lt=Emperor Higashiyama}}. Accompanying this, Fusako was bestowed the honorary name Shinjōsaimon-in. In early 1695,{{efn|The 7th month of {{ill|Genroku|en|Genroku|WD=Q1325001|lt=Genroku}} 8.}} the [[Tokugawa shogunate|shogunate]] presented her with 1,000 ''koku'' of land.
Takatsukasa Fusako died on May 19, 1712.{{efn|The 14th day of the 4th month of {{ill|Shōtoku (era)|en|Shōtoku (era)|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shōtoku}} 4.}} Her grave is located at {{ill|Tsuki no wa no misasagi|en|Tsuki no wa no misasagi|WD=Q3541649|lt=Tsuki no wa no misasagi}} in {{ill|Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|en|Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1203862|lt=Higashiyama-ku}}, {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto}}.
==Notes== All Western dates calculated using Tsuchihashi's database via the [http://www.yukikurete.de/nengo_calc.htm Nengocalc] tool. {{notelist|30em}}

{{Nihongo|'''Takatsukasa Fusako'''|鷹司房子||October 12, 1653 – May 19, 1712}}, also known as {{Nihongo|'''Shinjōsaimon-in'''|新上西門院}}, was an empress consort of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. She was the consort of {{ill|Emperor Reigen|en|Emperor Reigen|WD=Q313808|lt=Emperor Reigen}}.
==Life== Her father was {{ill|Takatsukasa Norihira|en|Takatsukasa Norihira|WD=Q1742537|lt=Takatsukasa Norihira}}, who had the post of ''[[:en:Sadaijin|Sadaijin]]'' and the {{Nihongo|[[Ritsuryō#Establishment_of_court_Rank|junior first Rank]]|従一位|1=Junior First Rank}}. Her half-siblings by other mothers included the ''[[:en:Kampaku|Kampaku]]'' {{ill|Takatsukasa Fusasuke|en|Takatsukasa Fusasuke|WD=Q924830|lt=Takatsukasa Fusasuke}}, the ''[[:en:Sadaijin|Sadaijin]]'' {{ill|Kujō Kaneharu|en|Kujō Kaneharu|WD=Q3200258|lt=Kujō Kaneharu}}, and [[Takatsukasa Nobuko]], the wife of [[Tokugawa Tsunayoshi]]. Another theory holds that Fusako was actually Norihira's younger sister, whom he adopted as his daughter.
===Marriage=== On December 1, 1670,{{efn|The 21st day of the 11th month of {{ill|Kanbun (era)|en|Kanbun (era)|WD=Q1146228|lt=Kanbun}} 9.}} Fusako entered the court of Emperor Reigen, one year her junior, as a court lady. On June 23, 1673,{{efn|The 9th day of the 5th month of Kanbun 13.}} the palace went up in flames, and the estate of ''[[:en:Udaijin|Udaijin]]'' {{ill|Konoe Motohiro|en|Konoe Motohiro|WD=Q922862|lt=Konoe Motohiro}} was used as a temporary palace. This was not a new occurrence: in 1661,{{efn|The 1st month of {{ill|Manji (era)|en|Manji (era)|WD=Q1145295|lt=Manji}} 4.}} during the reign of the previous emperor {{ill|Emperor Go-Sai|en|Emperor Go-Sai|WD=Q349414|lt=Emperor Go-Sai}}, another conflagration had led to the use of Motohiro's estate. On October 3, 1673,{{efn|The 23rd day of the 8th month of Kanbun 13.}} Fusako gave birth to her daughter {{Nihongo|Princess Masako|栄子内親王}}. In light of the great fire in {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto}} that had destroyed the palace, the {{ill|Japanese era name|en|Japanese era name|WD=Q24706|lt=era name}} was changed to {{ill|Enpō|en|Enpō|WD=Q1145735|lt=Enpō}}. But on January 10, 1676,{{efn|The 25th day of the 11th month of Enpō 3.}} immediately after a new palace had been completed, the temporary one at Motohiro's estate caught flame in turn. Fusako and the emperor found refuge at the home of {{Nihongo|Yoshida Kaneyuki|吉田兼敬}} before entering the new palace two days later.
===Empress=== On January 1, 1683,{{efn|The 7th day of the 12th month of {{ill|Tenna|en|Tenna|WD=Q1145289|lt=Tenna}} 2.}} Fusako was proclaimed {{Nihongo||准三后|jusangō}}, and on December 3{{efn|The 14th day of the 2nd month of Tenna 3.}} she was invested as ''[[:en:Chūgū|Chūgū]]''. She thereby became the emperor's legitimate wife—during the entire {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, this only happened four times. Reigen abdicated the throne on May 2, 1687,{{efn|The 21st day of the 3rd month of {{ill|Jōkyō|en|Jōkyō|WD=Q1146221|lt=Jōkyō}} 4.}} in favor of Crown Prince Asahito, who then became {{ill|Emperor Higashiyama|en|Emperor Higashiyama|WD=Q313801|lt=Emperor Higashiyama}}. Accompanying this, Fusako was bestowed the honorary name Shinjōsaimon-in. In early 1695,{{efn|The 7th month of {{ill|Genroku|en|Genroku|WD=Q1325001|lt=Genroku}} 8.}} the [[Tokugawa shogunate|shogunate]] presented her with 1,000 ''koku'' of land.
Takatsukasa Fusako died on May 19, 1712.{{efn|The 14th day of the 4th month of {{ill|Shōtoku (era)|en|Shōtoku (era)|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shōtoku}} 4.}} Her grave is located at {{ill|Tsuki no wa no misasagi|en|Tsuki no wa no misasagi|WD=Q3541649|lt=Tsuki no wa no misasagi}} in {{ill|Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|en|Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1203862|lt=Higashiyama-ku}}, {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto}}.

{{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|WD=Q205662}}|title={{ill|Empress of Japan|WD=Q595038|1=Empress of Japan}}|spouse={{ill|Emperor Reigen|WD=Q313808|1=Emperor Reigen}}|children={{ill|Masako-naishinnō|WD=Q7142051|1=Masako-naishinnō}}|father={{ill|Takatsukasa Norihira|WD=Q1742537|1=Takatsukasa Norihira}}|mother={{ill|Reizei Tamemitsus dotter|WD=Q109303450|1=Reizei Tamemitsus dotter}}|relatives={{ill|Kujō Kaneharu|WD=Q3200258|1=Kujō Kaneharu}}; {{ill|Takatsukasa Fusasuke|WD=Q924830|1=Takatsukasa Fusasuke}}; {{ill|Takatsukasa Nobuko|WD=Q7140666|1=Takatsukasa Nobuko}}|given_name={{ill|Fusako|WD=Q5509874|1=Fusako}}|resting_place={{ill|Tsuki no wa no misasagi|WD=Q3541649|1=Tsuki no wa no misasagi}}}} {{family name hatnote|Takatsukasa|lang=Japanese}} {{Infobox royalty | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | succession = {{ill|Empress of Japan|en|Empress of Japan|WD=Q595038|lt=Empress of Japan|1=Empress of Japan}} | reign = 1683–1687 | birth_name = | birth_date = {{birth date|1653|10|12|df=y}} | birth_place = | death_date = {{death date and age|1712|05|19|1653|10|12|df=y}} | death_place = | burial_date = | burial_place = Tsuki no wa no misasagi | spouse = {{marriage|{{ill|Emperor Reigen|en|Emperor Reigen|WD=Q313808|lt=Emperor Reigen|1=Emperor Reigen}}|December 1, 1670}} | consort = yes | issue = | issue-link = | issue-pipe = | issue-type = | full name = | era name = | era dates = | regnal name = | posthumous name= | temple name = | royal house = {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=Imperial House of Japan|1=Imperial House of Japan}} | father = Takatsukasa Norihira | mother = | religion = | occupation = | signature_type = | signature =
| module = }}
{{Nihongo|'''Takatsukasa Fusako'''|鷹司房子||October 12, 1653 – May 19, 1712}}, also known as {{Nihongo|'''Shinjōsaimon-in'''|新上西門院}}, was an empress consort of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. She was the consort of {{ill|Emperor Reigen|en|Emperor Reigen|WD=Q313808|lt=Emperor Reigen|1=Emperor Reigen}}.
==Life== Her father was {{ill|Takatsukasa Norihira|en|Takatsukasa Norihira|WD=Q1742537|lt=Takatsukasa Norihira|1=Takatsukasa Norihira}}, who had the post of ''[[:en:Sadaijin|Sadaijin]]'' and the {{Nihongo|[[Ritsuryō#Establishment_of_court_Rank|junior first Rank]]|従一位|1=Junior First Rank}}. Her half-siblings by other mothers included the ''[[:en:Kampaku|Kampaku]]'' {{ill|Takatsukasa Fusasuke|en|Takatsukasa Fusasuke|WD=Q924830|lt=Takatsukasa Fusasuke|1=Takatsukasa Fusasuke}}, the ''[[:en:Sadaijin|Sadaijin]]'' {{ill|Kujō Kaneharu|en|Kujō Kaneharu|WD=Q3200258|lt=Kujō Kaneharu|1=Kujō Kaneharu}}, and [[Takatsukasa Nobuko]], the wife of [[Tokugawa Tsunayoshi]]. Another theory holds that Fusako was actually Norihira's younger sister, whom he adopted as his daughter.
===Marriage=== On December 1, 1670,{{efn|The 21st day of the 11th month of {{ill|Kanbun (era)|en|Kanbun (era)|WD=Q1146228|lt=Kanbun|1=Kanbun (era)}} 9.}} Fusako entered the court of Emperor Reigen, one year her junior, as a court lady. On June 23, 1673,{{efn|The 9th day of the 5th month of Kanbun 13.}} the palace went up in flames, and the estate of ''[[:en:Udaijin|Udaijin]]'' {{ill|Konoe Motohiro|en|Konoe Motohiro|WD=Q922862|lt=Konoe Motohiro|1=Konoe Motohiro}} was used as a temporary palace. This was not a new occurrence: in 1661,{{efn|The 1st month of {{ill|Manji (era)|en|Manji (era)|WD=Q1145295|lt=Manji|1=Manji (era)}} 4.}} during the reign of the previous emperor {{ill|Emperor Go-Sai|en|Emperor Go-Sai|WD=Q349414|lt=Emperor Go-Sai|1=Emperor Go-Sai}}, another conflagration had led to the use of Motohiro's estate. On October 3, 1673,{{efn|The 23rd day of the 8th month of Kanbun 13.}} Fusako gave birth to her daughter {{Nihongo|Princess Masako|栄子内親王}}. In light of the great fire in {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto|1=Kyoto}} that had destroyed the palace, the {{ill|Japanese era name|en|Japanese era name|WD=Q24706|lt=era name|1=Japanese era name}} was changed to {{ill|Enpō|en|Enpō|WD=Q1145735|lt=Enpō|1=Enpō}}. But on January 10, 1676,{{efn|The 25th day of the 11th month of Enpō 3.}} immediately after a new palace had been completed, the temporary one at Motohiro's estate caught flame in turn. Fusako and the emperor found refuge at the home of {{Nihongo|Yoshida Kaneyuki|吉田兼敬}} before entering the new palace two days later.
===Empress=== On January 1, 1683,{{efn|The 7th day of the 12th month of {{ill|Tenna|en|Tenna|WD=Q1145289|lt=Tenna|1=Tenna}} 2.}} Fusako was proclaimed {{Nihongo||准三后|jusangō}}, and on December 3{{efn|The 14th day of the 2nd month of Tenna 3.}} she was invested as ''[[:en:Chūgū|Chūgū]]''. She thereby became the emperor's legitimate wife—during the entire {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, this only happened four times. Reigen abdicated the throne on May 2, 1687,{{efn|The 21st day of the 3rd month of {{ill|Jōkyō|en|Jōkyō|WD=Q1146221|lt=Jōkyō|1=Jōkyō}} 4.}} in favor of Crown Prince Asahito, who then became {{ill|Emperor Higashiyama|en|Emperor Higashiyama|WD=Q313801|lt=Emperor Higashiyama|1=Emperor Higashiyama}}. Accompanying this, Fusako was bestowed the honorary name Shinjōsaimon-in. In early 1695,{{efn|The 7th month of {{ill|Genroku|en|Genroku|WD=Q1325001|lt=Genroku|1=Genroku}} 8.}} the [[Tokugawa shogunate|shogunate]] presented her with 1,000 ''koku'' of land.
Takatsukasa Fusako died on May 19, 1712.{{efn|The 14th day of the 4th month of {{ill|Shōtoku (era)|en|Shōtoku (era)|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shōtoku|1=Shōtoku (era)}} 4.}} Her grave is located at {{ill|Tsuki no wa no misasagi|en|Tsuki no wa no misasagi|WD=Q3541649|lt=Tsuki no wa no misasagi|1=Tsuki no wa no misasagi}} in {{ill|Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|en|Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1203862|lt=Higashiyama-ku|1=Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto}}, {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto|1=Kyoto}}.
==Notes== All Western dates calculated using Tsuchihashi's database via the [http://www.yukikurete.de/nengo_calc.htm Nengocalc] tool. {{notelist|30em}}

{{ill|Takatsukasa Fusasuke|WD=Q924830|1=Takatsukasa Fusasuke}}; {{ill|Takatsukasa Nobuko|WD=Q7140666|1=Takatsukasa Nobuko}}|given_name={{ill|Fusako|WD=Q5509874|1=Fusako}}|resting_place={{ill|Tsuki no wa no misasagi|WD=Q3541649|1=Tsuki no wa no misasagi}}}} {{family name hatnote|Takatsukasa|lang=Japanese}} {{Infobox royalty | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | succession = {{ill|Empress of Japan|en|Empress of Japan|WD=Q595038|lt=Empress of Japan|1=Empress of Japan}} | reign = 1683–1687 | birth_name = | birth_date = {{birth date|1653|10|12|df=y}} | birth_place = | death_date = {{death date and age|1712|05|19|1653|10|12|df=y}} | death_place = | burial_date = | burial_place = Tsuki no wa no misasagi | spouse = {{marriage|{{ill|Emperor Reigen|en|Emperor Reigen|WD=Q313808|lt=Emperor Reigen|1=Emperor Reigen}}|December 1, 1670}} | consort = yes | issue = | issue-link = | issue-pipe = | issue-type = | full name = | era name = | era dates = | regnal name = | posthumous name= | temple name = | royal house = {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=Imperial House of Japan|1=Imperial House of Japan}} | father = Takatsukasa Norihira | mother = | religion = | occupation = | signature_type = | signature =
| module = }}
{{Nihongo|'''Takatsukasa Fusako'''|鷹司房子||October 12, 1653 – May 19, 1712}}, also known as {{Nihongo|'''Shinjōsaimon-in'''|新上西門院}}, was an empress consort of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. She was the consort of {{ill|Emperor Reigen|en|Emperor Reigen|WD=Q313808|lt=Emperor Reigen|1=Emperor Reigen}}.
==Life== Her father was {{ill|Takatsukasa Norihira|en|Takatsukasa Norihira|WD=Q1742537|lt=Takatsukasa Norihira|1=Takatsukasa Norihira}}, who had the post of ''[[:en:Sadaijin|Sadaijin]]'' and the {{Nihongo|[[Ritsuryō#Establishment_of_court_Rank|junior first Rank]]|従一位|1=Junior First Rank}}. Her half-siblings by other mothers included the ''[[:en:Kampaku|Kampaku]]'' {{ill|Takatsukasa Fusasuke|en|Takatsukasa Fusasuke|WD=Q924830|lt=Takatsukasa Fusasuke|1=Takatsukasa Fusasuke}}, the ''[[:en:Sadaijin|Sadaijin]]'' {{ill|Kujō Kaneharu|en|Kujō Kaneharu|WD=Q3200258|lt=Kujō Kaneharu|1=Kujō Kaneharu}}, and [[Takatsukasa Nobuko]], the wife of [[Tokugawa Tsunayoshi]]. Another theory holds that Fusako was actually Norihira's younger sister, whom he adopted as his daughter.
===Marriage=== On December 1, 1670,{{efn|The 21st day of the 11th month of {{ill|Kanbun (era)|en|Kanbun (era)|WD=Q1146228|lt=Kanbun|1=Kanbun (era)}} 9.}} Fusako entered the court of Emperor Reigen, one year her junior, as a court lady. On June 23, 1673,{{efn|The 9th day of the 5th month of Kanbun 13.}} the palace went up in flames, and the estate of ''[[:en:Udaijin|Udaijin]]'' {{ill|Konoe Motohiro|en|Konoe Motohiro|WD=Q922862|lt=Konoe Motohiro|1=Konoe Motohiro}} was used as a temporary palace. This was not a new occurrence: in 1661,{{efn|The 1st month of {{ill|Manji (era)|en|Manji (era)|WD=Q1145295|lt=Manji|1=Manji (era)}} 4.}} during the reign of the previous emperor {{ill|Emperor Go-Sai|en|Emperor Go-Sai|WD=Q349414|lt=Emperor Go-Sai|1=Emperor Go-Sai}}, another conflagration had led to the use of Motohiro's estate. On October 3, 1673,{{efn|The 23rd day of the 8th month of Kanbun 13.}} Fusako gave birth to her daughter {{Nihongo|Princess Masako|栄子内親王}}. In light of the great fire in {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto|1=Kyoto}} that had destroyed the palace, the {{ill|Japanese era name|en|Japanese era name|WD=Q24706|lt=era name|1=Japanese era name}} was changed to {{ill|Enpō|en|Enpō|WD=Q1145735|lt=Enpō|1=Enpō}}. But on January 10, 1676,{{efn|The 25th day of the 11th month of Enpō 3.}} immediately after a new palace had been completed, the temporary one at Motohiro's estate caught flame in turn. Fusako and the emperor found refuge at the home of {{Nihongo|Yoshida Kaneyuki|吉田兼敬}} before entering the new palace two days later.
===Empress=== On January 1, 1683,{{efn|The 7th day of the 12th month of {{ill|Tenna|en|Tenna|WD=Q1145289|lt=Tenna|1=Tenna}} 2.}} Fusako was proclaimed {{Nihongo||准三后|jusangō}}, and on December 3{{efn|The 14th day of the 2nd month of Tenna 3.}} she was invested as ''[[:en:Chūgū|Chūgū]]''. She thereby became the emperor's legitimate wife—during the entire {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, this only happened four times. Reigen abdicated the throne on May 2, 1687,{{efn|The 21st day of the 3rd month of {{ill|Jōkyō|en|Jōkyō|WD=Q1146221|lt=Jōkyō|1=Jōkyō}} 4.}} in favor of Crown Prince Asahito, who then became {{ill|Emperor Higashiyama|en|Emperor Higashiyama|WD=Q313801|lt=Emperor Higashiyama|1=Emperor Higashiyama}}. Accompanying this, Fusako was bestowed the honorary name Shinjōsaimon-in. In early 1695,{{efn|The 7th month of {{ill|Genroku|en|Genroku|WD=Q1325001|lt=Genroku|1=Genroku}} 8.}} the [[Tokugawa shogunate|shogunate]] presented her with 1,000 ''koku'' of land.
Takatsukasa Fusako died on May 19, 1712.{{efn|The 14th day of the 4th month of {{ill|Shōtoku (era)|en|Shōtoku (era)|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shōtoku|1=Shōtoku (era)}} 4.}} Her grave is located at {{ill|Tsuki no wa no misasagi|en|Tsuki no wa no misasagi|WD=Q3541649|lt=Tsuki no wa no misasagi|1=Tsuki no wa no misasagi}} in {{ill|Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|en|Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1203862|lt=Higashiyama-ku|1=Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto}}, {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto|1=Kyoto}}.
==Notes== All Western dates calculated using Tsuchihashi's database via the [http://www.yukikurete.de/nengo_calc.htm Nengocalc] tool. {{notelist|30em}}
